metadata.working_title: Glutamate neurotransmission from leptin receptor cells in the ventral premammillary nucleus is required for female sexual maturation and reproduction

abstract_sketch.problem: Leptin is a permissive metabolic signal that enables pubertal maturation and fertility through leptin receptor (LepRb) neurons of the ventral premammillary nucleus (PMv), but how these neurons relay the signal to the reproductive axis remains unresolved.
abstract_sketch.gap: PMv LepRb neurons are largely glutamatergic, yet whether glutamate is the effector that drives GnRH/LH output, pubertal timing and adult fertility has not been tested causally.
abstract_sketch.approach: We combined chemogenetic activation of PMv LepRb neurons with automated serial blood sampling for luteinizing hormone (LH), kisspeptin challenge, conditional deletion of vesicular glutamate transporter 2 (Vglut2) in LepRb cells (LepRbDeltaVglut2), and virally-mediated simultaneous re-expression of LepR and deletion of Vglut2 selectively in the PMv of LepR-null (LepRloxTB) mice.
abstract_sketch.key_results: Chemogenetic activation of PMv LepRb neurons induced LH release within 10-20 min in 55% of CNO-injected hits (8/15) and in 100% of clozapine-injected hits (4/4), with LH peaks tightly correlated with cFos counts in the PMv (Pearson r=0.67, p=0.0007). LepRbDeltaVglut2 females showed prolonged estrous cycles (4.47+/-0.30 vs 3.31+/-0.36 cycles/30d, t26=2.45, p=0.021), reduced pituitary response to kisspeptin-10 (AUC t11=3.14, p=0.009), reduced Arc GnRH-ir (t5=8.01, p=0.0005) and 50% lower hypothalamic Tac2 (t8=3.39, p=0.009). Re-expressing LepR in PMv of LepR-null mice while simultaneously deleting Vglut2 failed to rescue puberty or fertility, despite restored leptin-induced pSTAT3 (F3,13=73.53, p<0.0001).
abstract_sketch.impact: Glutamate is the indispensable neurotransmitter through which PMv LepRb neurons transmit the permissive metabolic signal of leptin to the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, identifying a circuit-level mechanism by which body energy stores gate reproduction.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Leptin links adiposity to reproductive competence; leptin or leptin-receptor deficiency causes hypogonadotropic hypogonadism in mice and humans
LepRb neurons are distributed across many hypothalamic nuclei, but PMv LepRb neurons are a dominant relay to GnRH circuits
Previous genetic and viral rescue studies established PMv LepRb as sufficient to restore puberty and fertility in LepR-null mice
About 75% of PMv LepRb neurons are depolarized by leptin and most are glutamatergic (Vglut2+)
Kiss1/KNDy neurons in the Arc express LepRb only rarely (about 10%), limiting their role as direct leptin sensors
Gap: whether glutamate is the effector transmitter is untested causally

Here we test (i) whether activating PMv LepRb neurons suffices to trigger LH, (ii) whether deleting Vglut2 from LepRb cells impairs puberty/fertility, and (iii) whether simultaneous LepR re-expression plus Vglut2 deletion in the PMv rescues fertility

subsections.title: Chemogenetic activation of PMv LepRb neurons induces LH release
content_bullets: Unilateral AAV-hM3Dq-mCherry in PMv of LepRb-Cre females followed by iv CNO (0.5 mg/kg) produces LH rise within 10-20 min in 55% (8/15) of hits; peak 0.65-3.11 ng/mL
Controls: PMv-miss (n=2) and AAV-mCherry (n=5) rarely show LH rise; no-AAV + clozapine (n=7) shows 28.5% spontaneous rise
One-sample t-tests: PMv-hit with LH increase t7=3.54 p=0.009; without increase t6=1.37 p=0.22; controls t6=1.00 p=0.36
cFos-ir in PMv: one-way ANOVA F2,19=81.66 p<0.0001; Pearson correlation with peak LH r=0.67 p=0.0007
Clozapine directly: 100% (4/4) hM3Dq-PMv-hit mice showed LH rise (0.87-1.18 ng/mL); t3=8.79 p=0.003
cFos in PMv (clozapine): t8=17.46 p<0.0001

subsections.title: Viral contamination of the posterior arcuate produces inconsistent LH secretion
content_bullets: Peak LH correlated with cFos+mCherry double-labeled cells in posterior Arc (Pearson r=0.62, p=0.002) but not with cFos alone (r=0.3, p=0.16)
POMC coexpression: 9.3+/-2.6% of Arc cFos (12.3+/-5.0 cells) in PMv-hit; ~50% also mCherry (7.3+/-3.2 cells) vs 0.3+/-0.3 in hM3Dq+clozapine
In LepRb-Cre;Kiss1-hrGFP mice, only 2.5+/-0.95% of posterior Arc cFos cells were Kiss1-hrGFP+ (1.8+/-0.3 cells); most coexpressed mCherry
Arc cFos+mCherry across groups: Kruskal-Wallis=14.63, p<0.0001

subsections.title: LepRbDeltaVglut2 mice develop late-onset obesity and impaired reproduction
content_bullets: Fat mass: males t9=3.77 p=0.004; females t11=3.7 p=0.0035
Vaginal opening: t18=0.84 p=0.41 (age); first estrus later: t14=2.45 p=0.028
Estrous cycle duration longer: t25=3.71, p=0.001; proestrus t26=2.81 p=0.009; diestrus t26=2.39 p=0.024
Cycles per 30 days: 4.47+/-0.30 (control) vs 3.31+/-0.36 (mutant), t26=2.45 p=0.021
Fertility: 75% LepRbDeltaVglut2 vs 100% controls produced a litter within 60 d; litter size 7.86+/-1.14 vs 7.50+/-1.41 (t11=0.20, p=0.85)

subsections.title: LepRbDeltaVglut2 females show reduced GnRH-ir and blunted response to kisspeptin
content_bullets: Arc GnRH-ir integrated density reduced: t5=8.01 p=0.0005
Basal LH: t10=1.91 p=0.08

LH after kisspeptin-10 (65 ug/kg ip): Mixed model time F1.14,12.19=15.91 p=0.001; genotype F1,11=8.26 p=0.015; interaction F3,32=5.00 p=0.006; Sidak post-hoc: 7 min t10.97=3.79 p=0.01; 14 min t9.4=3.22 p=0.04
AUC LH post-kisspeptin: t11=3.14 p=0.009
Pituitary qPCR: Gnrhr t10=0.86 p=0.41; LHb t10=1.47 p=0.17; FSHb t9=1.63 p=0.14; CGA Vglut2flox 100+/-17.47% vs LepRbDeltaVglut2 245.3+/-89.91%, t8=1.20 p=0.26
Mediobasal hypothalamus qPCR: Kiss1 t8=1.42 p=0.19; Pdyn t8=0.02 p=0.98; Kiss1r t8=1.44 p=0.19; Tac3r t8=0.99 p=0.35; Tac2 t8=3.39 p=0.009 (~50% reduction)

subsections.title: LepR re-expression in PMv requires glutamate for pubertal rescue and fertility
content_bullets: AAV-Cre in LepRloxTB re-induces leptin-induced pSTAT3 in PMv (F3,13=73.53, p<0.0001)
Same AAV-Cre in LepRloxTB;Vglut2flox deletes Vglut2 (t8=4.20 p=0.003) while restoring pSTAT3
Arc pSTAT3 not different between groups (F3,16=171.8 p<0.0001 overall)
Corpora lutea: F3,17=31.16 p<0.0001; no vaginal opening, no pregnancy in LepRloxTB;Vglut2flox injected correctly

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: Causal demonstration that PMv LepRb activation drives LH and that glutamate is required for leptin's permissive role on the reproductive axis
Dose-threshold hypothesis: not all activated PMv LepRb subsets release LH (about 55% CNO, 100% clozapine) -> subset/dose dependence
Contamination considerations: POMC/KNDy contamination unlikely to account for findings
Circuit interpretation: PMv LepRb glutamatergic drive activates upstream of kisspeptin, given blunted kisspeptin response in LepRbDeltaVglut2
Body weight interpretation: reproductive defects precede obesity (observed before 16 weeks)
Limitations: unilateral injections, AAV spread, lack of female-only direct PMv Vglut2 deletion without LepR manipulation
Future work: conditional PMv-specific Vglut2 deletion; male phenotype; cell-type-resolved glutamate output

sections.title: Methods
content_bullets: Mice: LepRb-Cre, LepRb-Cre;Vglut2flox, LepRloxTB, LepRloxTB;Vglut2flox, LepRb-Cre;Kiss1-hrGFP (n=3-15/group)
Stereotaxic AAV-hM3Dq-mCherry or AAV-Cre unilateral into PMv (AP -5.4 mm from rostral rhinal vein, ML +/-0.5 mm, DV -5.4 mm); 50 nL
Culex automated blood sampling: 3 baselines -30/-20/-10 min; iv CNO or clozapine 0.5 mg/kg in 50 uL; 10-min samples for 60 min
Kisspeptin-10: 65 ug/kg ip; 7-min samples for 30 min
Assays: in-house LH sandwich ELISA (functional sensitivity 0.016 ng/mL; range 0.016-4.0 ng/mL; intra-assay CV 6.24%; inter-assay CVs 7.3/5.0/6.5% for low/med/high QC)
Histology: perfusion; 30-um sections; IF (anti-cFos, anti-mCherry, anti-POMC, anti-GnRH, anti-pSTAT3); 35S-Vglut2 radioactive ISH; BaseScope Duplex (LepR green + Vglut2 fast-red)
qPCR: SYBR Green on CFX-384; normalized to Actb (pituitary) or B2m (MBH)

Statistics: one-sample t-tests against 0 for LH AUC; one-way and two-way ANOVA with post hocs; Kruskal-Wallis for non-normal; mixed-effects for repeated-measures LH; Pearson correlations; mean +/- SEM

must_cite: Donato et al 2011 PMv LepR rescue
Mahany et al 2018 PMv LepR fertility rescue
Xu et al 2013 Vglut2 LepR obesity
